game_overview: The Fox in the Forest is a trick-taking game for two players set in a fairy tale world. Use the special abilities of the fairy tale characters to change the trump suit, lead even after you lose a trick, and more. Score points by winning the right number of tricks each round - but don't get greedy! Win too many tricks and you'll fall prey to the curse of the forest.
objective: Be the first player to reach 21 points by winning the right number of tricks each round.

components_overview.name: Playing Cards
components_overview.description: 33 cards: 11 cards in each of three suits (Bells, Keys, Moons), numbered 1-11.
components_overview.detailed_description: Each suit contains cards ranked 1-11. Odd-numbered cards (1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11) feature fairy tale characters with special abilities. Even-numbered cards (2, 4, 6, 8, 10) have no special abilities.

components_overview.name: Scoring Tokens
components_overview.description: 17 tokens in denominations of 1, 3, and 6 points for tracking score.

components_overview.name: Player Reference Cards
components_overview.description: 2 double-sided reference cards showing the scoring table and card abilities.

setup.description: Deal 13 cards face-down to each player. Keep your hand hidden from your opponent.
Place the remaining 7 cards face-down in the center of the table to form the draw deck.
Flip the top card of the draw deck face-up. This is the Decree Card and its suit is the trump suit for this round.
The non-dealer leads the first trick.

gameplay.turn_structure: The game is played over multiple rounds. Each round consists of 13 tricks. The trick winner leads the next trick (unless a Swan ability changes this).

actions.name: Lead a Trick
actions.description: The lead player plays any card from their hand face-up to the center.

steps.description: Choose any card from your hand.
Play it face-up to the center of the table. This card determines the lead suit.
If the card has a special ability, resolve it now.

actions.name: Follow a Trick
actions.description: The following player must play a card that matches the lead suit if possible.

steps.description: If you have a card matching the lead suit, you MUST play one of those cards.
If you have no cards of the lead suit, you may play any card from your hand.
If the card has a special ability, resolve it now.

actions.name: Determine Trick Winner
actions.description: Compare the two cards to determine who wins the trick.

steps.description: If a trump suit card was played, the highest trump card wins the trick.
If no trump was played, the highest card of the lead suit wins.
The winner takes both cards and places them face-down in their scoring pile.
If the trick contains any 7s (Treasure), the winner immediately scores 1 point for each 7 in the trick.

notes.description: A 9 (Witch) played alone acts as if it were in the trump suit.
If both players play a 9, they cancel out and act as their actual suits.

key_concepts.name: Trump Suit
key_concepts.description: The suit of the Decree Card is the trump suit. Trump cards beat any card of another suit, regardless of rank.

key_concepts.name: Following Suit
key_concepts.description: You must play a card of the lead suit if you have one. If you cannot follow suit, you may play any card (including trump to win, or discarding an unwanted card).

key_concepts.name: Special Abilities
key_concepts.description: Odd-numbered cards (1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11) have special abilities that activate when played. Even-numbered cards have no abilities.

key_concepts.name: Greedy vs Humble
key_concepts.description: Winning too many tricks (10+) is 'Greedy' and scores 0 points. Winning very few tricks (0-3) is 'Humble' and scores the maximum 6 points. The sweet spot is 7-9 tricks for 6 points.

end_game.trigger: The game ends immediately when either player has 21 or more points at the end of a round.
procedure: After all 13 tricks are played, count the tricks each player won.
Award points according to the scoring table.
If neither player has 21+ points, shuffle all cards and deal a new round.
Alternate who deals each round (non-dealer leads the first trick).

final_scoring.description: Score 6 points. You avoided falling prey to the curse of the forest.
Score 6 points. You achieved victory through skill.
Score 0 points. Your greed has caught up with you!
During play, winning a trick with a 7 scores 1 bonus point per 7 in that trick.

winning_condition: The player with the most points when the game ends wins. In case of a tie, the player who scored more points in the final round wins. Game length variants: 16 points (shorter game) or 35 points (longer game).
